claims.claim_text: NASA's lessons-learned approach inspired Google's SRE culture, and NASA's database goes back to the 1960s including tragedy-related failures.
claims.anchor_text: **NASA's approach to postmortems inspired Google's SRE culture**. NASA's "lessons learned" database has entries going back to the 1960s, including failures that led to tragedies.
claims.suggested_rewrite: High-risk engineering organizations have long used lessons-learned programs and failure analysis to capture incidents and feed them back into future engineering work.
claims.rationale: The NASA-to-Google lineage and the 1960s/tragedy specifics are too specific to leave in place without a precise primary source that directly states them.

claims.claim_text: The aviation industry uses blameless reporting culture, including anonymous mistake reporting, and that practice made flying remarkably safe.
claims.anchor_text: **The aviation industry's blameless culture has made flying remarkably safe**. Pilots can report mistakes anonymously, leading to systemic improvements. SRE borrowed this concept.
claims.suggested_rewrite: Aviation has long used confidential, non-punitive incident reporting and investigation practices to improve safety, and SRE borrowed similar ideas.
claims.rationale: The anonymous-reporting practice is real, but the sentence's safety superlative and broad causal claim are too strong as written.

claims.claim_text: John Allspaw, described here as former Etsy CTO, popularized blameless postmortems in tech via a 2012 talk titled "Blameless PostMortems."
claims.anchor_text: **John Allspaw (former Etsy CTO) popularized "blameless postmortems" in tech**. His 2012 talk "Blameless PostMortems" is considered a foundational SRE resource.
claims.suggested_rewrite: John Allspaw helped popularize blameless postmortems in web operations through Etsy's 2012 writing and later talks.
claims.rationale: Allspaw's influence is real, but the exact job-title, talk-title, and "foundational resource" wording are not reliable enough as written.
Cloudflare maintains a public post-mortem archive on an allowlisted incident domain.
Google Cloud publishes public incident reports on its allowlisted service health domain.

claims.claim_text: GitLab's handbook includes a public incident review page and template workflow.
claims.rationale: The exact public handbook page directly supports the claim, but the host is outside the current allowlist.

claims.claim_text: Public postmortem transparency builds customer trust and contributes to industry-wide learning.
claims.anchor_text: This radical transparency builds customer trust and contributes to industry-wide learning.
claims.suggested_rewrite: Publishing postmortems publicly can improve customer communication and let other teams learn from the incident.
claims.rationale: The claimed outcome is plausible but too absolute to leave as a hard fact without direct evidence.

claims.claim_text: The anecdote involved a 4-hour outage during peak traffic.
claims.suggested_rewrite: - A major outage during a busy period
claims.rationale: The duration is false-precise and part of an unattributed anecdote.

claims.claim_text: The anecdote says the outage was caused by an engineer deploying an untested change.
claims.anchor_text: - Cause: An engineer deployed a change that wasn't tested
claims.suggested_rewrite: - Cause: An untested change reached production
claims.rationale: This is presented as a real incident cause inside an unattributed anecdote and should be generalized.

claims.claim_text: The anecdote claims roughly $500K in revenue loss.
claims.rationale: The exact loss figure is unsupported and cannot be honestly backfilled from a missing primary source.

claims.claim_text: Six months later there were zero similar incidents.
claims.span_hint: section: War Story / Six Months Later
claims.suggested_rewrite: - Fewer repeat incidents of the same type
claims.rationale: This is a precise outcome statistic from an unattributed anecdote.

claims.span_hint: section: War Story / Six Months Later
claims.rationale: This is a precise performance statistic from an unattributed anecdote.

claims.claim_text: Writing a good postmortem takes 2-4 hours.
claims.span_hint: section: Making Postmortems Effective / Time Investment
claims.suggested_rewrite: - Writing a solid postmortem takes focused time
claims.rationale: This is a house estimate, not a universal benchmark with a clear primary source.

claims.span_hint: section: Making Postmortems Effective / Time Investment
claims.suggested_rewrite: - The review meeting also takes dedicated time
claims.rationale: This is guidance, not a citable benchmark that holds across organizations.

claims.claim_text: "The engineer who deployed this is not the problem."
claims.span_hint: section: War Story / What Actually Happened
claims.anchor_text: The engineer who deployed this is not the problem.
claims.suggested_rewrite: The discussion emphasized that the delivery system, not a single engineer, needed to change.
claims.rationale: This is a verbatim attributed quote inside an unsourced anecdote.

claims.claim_text: "The engineer who deploys the NEXT untested change is also not the problem."
claims.span_hint: section: War Story / What Actually Happened
claims.anchor_text: The engineer who deploys the NEXT untested change is also not the problem.
claims.suggested_rewrite: The larger issue was that similar unsafe changes could still reach production unless the system changed.
claims.rationale: This is another verbatim quote presented as a real statement without a source.

claims.claim_text: The closing quotation is presented verbatim with no identifiable source.
claims.anchor_text: *"We are not the sum of our accidents. We are the sum of what we learn from them."* — Unknown
claims.suggested_rewrite: Learning from failure matters more than pretending failure can be avoided entirely.
claims.rationale: An unattributed quotation should be replaced with original prose unless a real source is supplied.

further_reading.title: Cloudflare Post Mortem Archive
further_reading.why_relevant: Public examples of incident writeups, timelines, and remediation follow-through.

further_reading.title: Google Cloud Incident Report
further_reading.why_relevant: Shows a public incident report with summary, root cause, and prevention steps.

further_reading.title: Postmortem Templates
further_reading.why_relevant: Useful reference set for structuring postmortems, action items, and review sections.

notes: Did not seed fictional quiz/exercise scenarios, template placeholders, or teaching questions. No shared-pool source directly covered the historical/vendor examples in this module.
